meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Build a Portfolio: Marketing Automation via AI Agents
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about no portfolio. Learn to build professional marketing automation tools using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate tutorial.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, build a high-impact marketing automation tool. By integrating Antigravity for workflow orchestration and Claude Code for intelligent script generation, you demonstrate technical seniority. This project proves you can ship autonomous, revenue-generating code, transforming your GitHub from a student repo into a professional showcase.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the ultimate barrier between you and your first engineering role. You have the theoretical knowledge, but the industry demands evidence of production-grade, autonomous systems. It is time to stop building 'to-do' apps and start engineering solutions that solve real business pain points.

Marketing automation is the perfect bridge. It is complex, highly visible, and demonstrates your ability to integrate LLMs with business logic. By mastering these tools, you aren't just writing code; you are architecting an engine that saves time and creates value, proving to hiring managers that you possess the mindset of a senior developer.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Identify a specific marketing task, such as automated social media sentiment analysis and response generation. We need to map the data flow from trigger to action.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Design a workflow for a marketing automation tool that monitors RSS feeds, analyzes content sentiment using LLMs, and drafts relevant LinkedIn posts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a project scaffold for a marketing automation engine using Python and Pydantic'.

steps.title: Set Up the Antigravity Orchestrator
steps.description: Configure the core engine that handles the execution of your marketing tasks. This ensures your code is modular and scalable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the core orchestration logic for an Antigravity workflow that handles retries, state management, and logging for external API calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --file main.py --change 'Implement Antigravity workflow orchestration for task scheduling'.

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Content Filtering
steps.description: Use Claude Code to integrate LLM logic that filters incoming marketing data for high-relevance signals before drafting responses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a function that uses an LLM to evaluate the sentiment and business relevance of marketing data, returning a boolean flag for processing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Write a content filtering module with LLM-based scoring'.

steps.title: Automate Content Generation
steps.description: Build the drafting engine that takes filtered signals and turns them into human-like marketing copy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a prompt-chaining module that takes filtered content and generates a professional marketing response optimized for LinkedIn engagement.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --file generator.py --change 'Add content generation logic using prompt templates'.

steps.title: Testing and Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Ensure your automation is robust by adding unit tests and configuring environment variables for secure API access.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of pytest unit tests for the marketing automation core modules, ensuring 80% coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Run unit tests and generate a documentation README for the project'.

pro_tips: Always implement local logging for LLM prompts; debugging your AI's 'thought process' is the most valuable skill in production.
Use structured output schemas (JSON mode) for all LLM responses to ensure your automation pipeline doesn't break on malformed text.
Treat your environment variables as first-class citizens; never hardcode keys, even in a portfolio project.
Add a 'Human-in-the-Loop' flag to your automation to show employers you understand the safety requirements of enterprise AI systems.
Focus your README on the 'Why'—document the business problem you solved, not just the technical stack you used.
content.next_steps: After completing this project, deploy it to a staging environment and record a 60-second Loom video demonstrating the automation in action. Link this video in your GitHub README to turn your code into a story.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to build production-grade marketing automation that separates you from the crowd of entry-level applicants. The only thing missing is the execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
